children.text: The UHF emergency frequency is:
The call of urgency should include

answers: PAN PAN (spoken 3 times), ALL STATIONS or addressed station (spoken 3 times)
correctAnswer: PAN PAN (spoken 3 times), ALL STATIONS or addressed station (spoken 3 times)

children.text: What are the phonetic letters for GRLF?
A penalty of $5000 and/or a year in jail will result:

answers: From not using a radio log.
From giving a false distress signal.
From not having a radio licence and talking on the radio.
correctAnswer: From giving a false distress signal

children.text: If you make a transmission in error the proper term to correct the statement is:
PAN PAN spoken 3X means:
Before making a test transmission:

answers: Listen to make sure you won't intervene with ongoing communications, then make a short 10 second or less transmission.
correctAnswer: Listen to make sure you won't intervene with ongoing communications, then make a short 10 second or less transmission.

children.text: Radio station licences in Canada are issued by:
Assuming your aircraft registration is C-FGAC, how would you call another aircraft whose call sign is C-FBRD?
On initial contact with Canadian ATC the proper registration to be used is:
The frequency 121.5 MHz would be spoken:
The time of 1:30 A.M. expressed in a 24-hour clock would be:
To acknowledge a transmission you should say:
Why should you make it a practice to monitor 121.50 MHz after landing?

answers: To listen for downed aircraft in the vicinity or the airport.
To make sure that the ELT in your aircraft has not been accidentally triggered during landing.
To listen for any aircraft safety advisories.
To make sure your receiver is working.
correctAnswer: To make sure that the ELT in your aircraft has not been accidentally triggered during landing.

children.text: The correct way to acknowledge the receipt of a distress message from aircraft C-GISI is (assuming your station is C-GYZD):

answers: Mayday, C-GISI C-GISI C-GISI, this is C-GYZD C-GYZD C-GYZD, Mayday received, over.
Mayday, mayday, mayday, this is C-GYZD C-GYZD C-GYZD, silence Mayday, over.
Mayday, C-GISI C-GISI C-GISI, this is C-GYZD C-GYZD C-GYZD, roger Mayday, out.
Mayday, C-GYLU C-GYLD C-GYLD, received Mayday, out.
correctAnswer: Mayday, C-GISI C-GISI C-GISI, this is C-GYZD C-GYZD C-GYZD, Mayday received, over.

children.text: The order of priority for transmission in the aeronautical service (the top three from highest to lowest) is:

answers: Distress, flight safety and weather warnings.
Distress, urgency and communications relating to radio direction finding.
correctAnswer: Distress, urgency and communications relating to radio direction finding.

children.text: Conversations heard over the air:

answers: Must be identified in English only.
Must not exceed 3 minutes in length.
Should be passed on to others who may be interested.

children.text: Radios that transmit eligible for licensing in Canada must be:
Messages or transmissions that are not allowed are:

answers: Mixed plain language and coded groups.
False distress signals, profane language, and superfluous communications.
Messages of a personal nature to an addressee.
correctAnswer: False distress signals, profane language, and superfluous communications.

children.text: The Radiotelephone Operators Certificate is obtained by:
In a call sign or an aircraft station would be?
"MAYDAY" means:

answers: Normal communications can resume on a restricted basis
A safety message will follow a Communist holiday

children.text: The expression indicating a distress situation is:
Radio call signs should be sent:

answers: At least on the first call up
As quickly as possible to save air time
As often as necessary until a reply is received
correctAnswer: At least on the first call up

children.text: Before making any radio transmission you should:

answers: Adjust the squelch control to maximum
Listen first to ensure that the frequency is clear of other transmissions
correctAnswer: Listen first to ensure that the frequency is clear of other transmissions

children.text: How can you recognize the sound from an "E.L.T."?

answers: A varying tone heard on 121.5 MHz
A varying tone heard on 126.7 MHz
A steady tone heard on 122.2 MHz
A pre-recorded voice message heard on 121.5 KHz
correctAnswer: A varying tone heard on 121.5 MHz

children.text: $125.44 would be spoken over the radio as:

answers: Dollar one two five decimal four four
One hundred and twenty five and forty forty cents
One two five decimal four four
Can't be said over the radio
correctAnswer: Dollar one two five decimal four four

children.text: What is a correct radio procedure:
Your airplane registration is C-FDRY. How would you call C-FQLE?

answers: Charlie Foxtrot Quebec Lima Charlie this is Charlie Foxtrot Delta Romeo Yankee
Charlie Foxtrot Quebec Lima Charlie, how do you read?
Charlie Foxtrot Quebec Lima Charlie, come in
Charlie Foxtrot Quebec Lima Charlie, where are thou?
correctAnswer: Charlie Foxtrot Quebec Lima Charlie this is Charlie Foxtrot Delta Romeo Yankee

children.text: On the radio, time should be expressed in:
Affirmative means:

answers: We need to vote on it

children.text: Any person having become aware of a radio communication transmitted other than by a broadcasting station or an amateur station is bound by the provisions of the Radiocommunication Act to:

answers: Preserve the secrecy of the transmission
To enter details of the intercept in the station log
Not to make a tape recording of such communication
To report use of the frequency to the nearest Dept. of Communications District office
correctAnswer: Preserve the secrecy of the transmission

children.text: Signal checks should:

answers: Last as long as the operator wishes

children.text: The distinctive call sign issued to radio stations should be used:

answers: At least when initial contact is established
correctAnswer: At least when initial contact is established

children.text: The figure 600 would be transmitted as:
Using the ITU Phonetic alphabet, how would you spell the word BRAINS?

answers: Bravo Romeo Alpha India November Sierra
Baker Robert Able Italy New York Susan
Boston Roger Apple Ida Nancy Sugar
correctAnswer: Bravo Romeo Alpha India November Sierra

children.text: The term 'SAY AGAIN' means:

answers: I will relay message to you

children.text: What does the abbreviation 'E.L.T.' represent?
What is the aeronautical distress frequency?
What other search and rescue frequency is now commonly used in conjunction with 121.5 MHz?
What word would you use to tell another station to proceed with their message?
Which of the following expressions follows proper radiotelephone procedure?
Which type of communications are not permitted?
Who controls communications when you are in distress?

answers: The aeronautical ground or base station
The aircraft in distress unless it hands over control to another station
correctAnswer: The aircraft in distress unless it hands over control to another station

children.text: Radio operators shall not divulge the contents or existence of correspondence:

answers: Except to the addressee or to properly authorized government officials
Except messages of distress, urgency or safety, or messages addressed to all stations
Of any type without being subject to a fine of $25,000 or subject to 12 months imprisonment

children.text: The call sign used to identify a land station is:

answers: Whatever name or number is convenient
The name of the person operating the station

children.text: The international VHF aeronautical air-ground distress frequency is:
The proper expression for 'that is not correct' is:
The Radiotelephone Operator's certificate is valid for:

answers: As long as the pilot can talk
1 year and then must be renewed
2 years and then must be renewed
5 years and then must be renewed
correctAnswer: As long as the pilot can talk

children.text: On the readability scale, a level of 3 would be considered:
8:30 p.m. is expressed using the 24-hour clock
A radio message is categorized as a 'distress' call when

answers: a condition concerning the safety of the aircraft exists which does not require immediate assistance
an urgent weather report must be made

children.text: An aircraft asks you for a radio check and they are found to be perfectly readable. You would let them know you read them...
An aircraft experiences an engine failure while overhead a controlled airport. The aircraft operator, after troubleshooting and unable to restart, should transmit a MAYDAY report on which frequency?
An aircraft that is unable to establish communication on the air-ground frequency in use at the time should transmit their urgency message on

answers: any frequency where help may be found
correctAnswer: any frequency where help may be found

children.text: An expression used in radio communication meaning 'I repeat for clarity or emphasis' would be
An urgency message is preceded by the urgency signal three times.
ATC asks you how many pounds of fuel you have on board your aircraft. You have 2,300 pounds of fuel. Your transmission sounds like this:

answers: Two three hundred pounds of fuel...
Two three zero zero pounds of fuel...
Two thousand, three hundred pounds of fuel...
correctAnswer: Two three zero zero pounds of fuel...

children.text: ATC provides you with the next frequency to contact. For the frequency 118.0, they will read it to you in which way?
How is the phonetic alphabet letter 'Q' pronounced?
How should the transmission of the number '1' be pronounced?

If an aircraft is causing interference on a frequency where a mayday call was just made, it can be requested that this aircraft stop communicating on this frequency. If the aircraft in distress is Cessna C-FABC and the interfering traffic is Piper C-GABC, the imposition of silence would sound like?

answers: PIPER GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, THIS IS CESSNA FOXTROT ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, SEELONCE PLEASE, OUT
PIPER GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, THIS IS CESSNA FOXTROT ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, SEELONCE MAYDAY, OUT
PIPER GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, THIS IS CESSNA FOXTROT ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, SEELONCE FEENEE, OUT
PIPER GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, THIS IS CESSNA FOXTROT ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, SEELONCE S'IL VOUS PLAIT, OUT
correctAnswer: PIPER GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, THIS IS CESSNA FOXTROT ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, SEELONCE MAYDAY, OUT

children.text: In communications between aeronautical ground stations and aircraft stations, which station normally has communication control? In communications between aircraft stations, which station is the controlling station?

Place the following three aircraft radio operators into the order of transmission priority from highest to lowest:
C-FROG - 'Steinbach South traffic, Cessna Foxtrot, Romeo, Oscar, Golf is downwind for runway three six, touch and go.'
C-GOOD - 'Steinbach South traffic, Cessna Golf, Oscar, Oscar, Delta is declaring a low fuel advisory.'
C-GLAD - 'Steinbach South base, Cessna Golf. Lima. Aloha. Delta has a PIREP to make for the inflight visibility.'
Select the technically correct procedure for cancelling a distress message:

answers: MAYDAY, MAYDAY, MAYDAY, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, THIS IS PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, TIME 1720 ZULU, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, DISTRESS TRAFFIC ENDED. OUT

MAYDAY, MAYDAY, MAYDAY, ALL STATIONS, THIS IS PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, TIME 1720 ZULU, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, DISTRESS TRAFFIC ENDED. OUT

MAYDAY, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, THIS IS CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, TIME 1720 ZULU, CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, DISTRESS TRAFFIC ENDED

MAYDAY, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, THIS IS PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, TIME 1720 ZULU, PIPER CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, DISTRESS TRAFFIC ENDED. OUT
correctAnswer: MAYDAY, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, THIS IS CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, TIME 1720 ZULU, CHARLIE GOLF ALPHA BRAVO CHARLIE, DISTRESS TRAFFIC ENDED

children.text: The correct transmission of $123.45 over the radio is:

answers: One hundred twenty three dollars forty five cents
One two three decimal four five dollars
Dollars one two three decimal four five
One two three dollars four five cents
correctAnswer: Dollars one two three decimal four five

children.text: The use of profane or obscene language is liable, on summary conviction, in the case of an individual, to a fine not exceeding ___, imprisonment for a term not exceeding ___ months, or to both.

Two aircraft operators are having a conversation on 126.7 about how many fish they caught at the boat launch the other day. Meanwhile, a helicopter operator has experienced an engine failure and is having to perform an autorotation to land in a forested area. What should the helicopter operator do?

answers: Politely join the conversation and then casually mention you will be crashing soon
Take careful notes of where the good fishing was found
Wait till the conversation is finished before providing a MAYDAY report
Interrupt transmission of the other callers to provide a MAYDAY report
correctAnswer: Interrupt transmission of the other callers to provide a MAYDAY report

children.text: What is technically missing from the following distress call example? "MAYDAY, MAYDAY, MAYDAY, THIS IS X-RAY SIERRA TANGO, X-RAY SIERRA TANGO, X-RAY SIERRA TANGO. MAYDAY, X-RAY SIERRA TANGO POSITION 15 MILES EAST OF LUMSDEN VOR, ALTITUDE 5500 FEET. SICK PASSENGER, PROCEEDING TO REGINA AIRPORT. X-RAY SIERRA TANGO LANGU, 1 SOULS ON BOARD."

answers: What kind of sickness the passenger has
Who the call is being directed to
The aircraft manufacturer's name and full registration
How scared the pilot is feeling
correctAnswer: The aircraft manufacturer's name and full registration

children.text: When an operator wishes to establish communication with a specific station, the call sign of the station called will be repeated:

answers: Once if radio conditions are good

children.text: When station operators hear a call but are uncertain that the call is intended for their station, they should:

answers: Reply immediately using the words 'AIRCRAFT CALLING', the called station identification, 'YOU ARE UNREADABLE'
Reply immediately using the words 'STATION CALLING', the called station's identification, 'GO AHEAD'
Not reply until the call has been repeated and understood
Reply immediately using the words 'STATION CALLING', the called station's identification, and say the words 'SAY AGAIN' and 'OVER'
correctAnswer: Not reply until the call has been repeated and understood

children.text: When using the phonetic alphabet, the correct way to pronounce 'V' is:
Which of the following best represents the correct form to receive a MAYDAY call?

answers: MAYDAY, MAYDAY, MAYDAY, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, THIS IS TORONTO CENTER, TORONTO CENTER, TORONTO CENTER, RECEIVED MAYDAY
MAYDAY, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, THIS IS TORONTO CENTER, RECEIVED MAYDAY
MAYDAY, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, THIS IS TORONTO CENTER RECEIVED MAYDAY
MAYDAY, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, THIS IS TORONTO CENTER, TORONTO CENTER, TORONTO CENTER, RECEIVED MAYDAY
correctAnswer: MAYDAY, MAYDAY, MAYDAY, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, PIPER GOLF SIERRA QUEBEC ZULU, THIS IS TORONTO CENTER, TORONTO CENTER, TORONTO CENTER, RECEIVED MAYDAY

children.text: Which of the following methods of radio communications should be avoided?
You switch to a new frequency in order to make a position report with local area traffic. Before making your position report you should

answers: Listen for other transmissions that you might be interrupting
Say who you are calling and who you are immediately upon changing the frequency
Turn your radio volume to maximum to make sure everyone can hear you
correctAnswer: Listen for other transmissions that you might be interrupting
